# Ben Zachara — DevOps Engineer (Full Context)

> This is the extended LLM context file for zachara.dev. It contains the complete resume, project details, and technical background for Ben Zachara.

## About

Ben Zachara is an infrastructure-focused DevOps engineer with hands-on experience in container orchestration, infrastructure as code, CI/CD pipelines, and cloud-native architecture. Passionate about automation, reliability, and building systems that scale.

## Skills

### Containers & Orchestration
Kubernetes, Docker, K3s, Helm, containerd

### Infrastructure as Code
Terraform, CloudFormation, Ansible

### CI/CD
GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, ArgoCD

### Cloud Platforms
AWS, Oracle Cloud, CloudFlare

### Monitoring & Observability
Prometheus, Grafana, ELK Stack

### Scripting & Languages
Bash, Python, TypeScript, Go, YAML

## Experience

### DevOps Engineer — Self-Directed Projects (2024 — Present)
- Built and maintained K3s cluster on Ubuntu-based Oracle Cloud VPS with Traefik ingress and CloudFlare TLS
- Operated day-to-day infrastructure workflows from an Omarchy Linux desktop environment
- Designed CI/CD pipelines with GitHub Actions for container builds, GHCR publishing, and Helm deploys
- Implemented infrastructure as code with Terraform for AWS and LocalStack environments
- Deployed monitoring stack (Prometheus, Grafana) for cluster observability
- Containerized applications with Docker using multi-stage builds and security best practices

### Technical Support & Systems Administration (Previous Role)
- Managed Linux servers and networking infrastructure
- Automated routine tasks with Bash and Python scripts
- Troubleshot complex system issues across distributed environments

## Education & Certifications

### University of Information Technology and Management in Rzeszów
Expected March 2028

### Continuous Self-Directed Learning
Cloud architecture, Kubernetes administration, infrastructure as code, and DevOps practices through hands-on projects, labs, and industry documentation.

### Pursuing
AWS Solutions Architect · CKA (Certified Kubernetes Administrator)

## Projects

### zachara-dev
This portfolio site — built with SolidJS/SolidStart, pre-rendered to static HTML at build time. CI/CD via GitHub Actions builds a Docker image and deploys to K3s with Helm. Served through CloudFlare and Traefik ingress.
- Tech: SolidJS, TailwindCSS, Docker, Helm, GitHub Actions
- GitHub: https://github.com/benzac708/zachara-dev

### vps-infrastructure
Self-hosted Ubuntu Oracle Cloud VPS running K3s, serving multiple containerized applications. Managed from an Omarchy desktop workflow. Includes Traefik ingress with TLS, Prometheus and Grafana monitoring, automated backups, Tailscale mesh networking for secure CI/CD access, and scheduled maintenance with safe reboot automation.
- Tech: K3s, Traefik, Prometheus, Grafana, Tailscale, Oracle Cloud

### cloud-app
Full-stack cloud-native application deployed on K3s with Helm charts, health checks, and Traefik ingress routing. Demonstrates end-to-end container orchestration workflow.
- Tech: Kubernetes, Helm, Docker, Traefik, Python
- GitHub: https://github.com/benzac708/cloud-app

### aws-lab
AWS infrastructure lab environment for hands-on practice with cloud services. VPC networking, EC2 instances, S3 storage, IAM policies, and CloudFormation templates.
- Tech: AWS, CloudFormation, VPC, IAM, EC2
- GitHub: https://github.com/benzac708/aws-lab

### terraform-localstack
Terraform modules tested against LocalStack for local AWS development. Provisions S3 buckets, DynamoDB tables, Lambda functions, and API Gateway endpoints without cloud costs.
- Tech: Terraform, LocalStack, AWS, S3, Lambda
- GitHub: https://github.com/benzac708/terraform-localstack

## Infrastructure

This site is built with SolidJS/SolidStart (SSG), styled with TailwindCSS v4, and deployed on a K3s Kubernetes cluster on Oracle Cloud. Traffic flows through CloudFlare and Traefik ingress. CI/CD via GitHub Actions builds Docker images and deploys with Helm.

## Contact

- Email: bernard@zachara.dev
- GitHub: https://github.com/benzac708
- Website: https://zachara.dev
